source: Full read-only sweep of ALL leads in EasySocial business 4026 (labels/profiles for all; transcripts sampled for reply patterns).
note: This is a point-in-time snapshot for reference/seed. The live dashboard should compute these numbers fresh from the EasySocial API (see api/stats.js).
typical_client: TikTok lead, permanently employed (R8,500+ net), has a valid licence, banks with Capitec, but has a credit problem (blacklisted/debt review, or missed payments, or unsure). This is why the credit-advice replies (blacklisted / badcredit / debtreview / nocredit) are the highest-value automations, and why ~454 applications end declined.
operational_insight: Human agents' manual time goes mostly to (1) application-status updates ('still waiting for feedback', 'we received your documents') and (2) credit diagnostics ('why is your score 580?', 'when was the last missed payment?'). Per business decision these pipeline/pre-approved/validated chats are handled by a human, so the bot escalates them rather than answering.
data_quality_notes: Bank field is free-text with case/spelling variants (Capitec/Capitac, FNB/Fnb) and a few numbers leaked in — normalize in the dashboard.
Self-employed minimum income differs across templates (R14k/R15k/R20k/R25k) — confirm the correct figure.
